About the Advocate

Advocate Harish Kumar

With over two decades of dedicated practice, I have represented clients across a wide spectrum of legal matters — from complex civil disputes to criminal defense, family law, and property issues. My approach is built on clarity, diligence, and unwavering commitment to my clients' interests.

I believe every person deserves to understand their legal rights. Whether you are facing a court matter or simply need guidance, I am here to provide honest, practical counsel.

  • M.Com, LLB — Delhi University
  • Enrolled with Bar Council of Delhi, Reg. No. D/543/2002
  • Member, District Court Bar Association, Shahdara
  • Practice areas include Civil, Criminal, Family & Property Law
Expertise

Practice Areas

Handling a broad range of legal matters with focused expertise and personal attention to every case.

⚖️
Civil Litigation

Property disputes, contract enforcement, recovery suits, injunctions, and civil appeals across trial and appellate courts.

🏛️
Criminal Defense

Bail applications, FIR matters, anticipatory bail, trial defense, and representation in criminal proceedings.

👨‍👩‍👧
Family Law

Divorce, maintenance, child custody, matrimonial disputes, and domestic relations handled with sensitivity and care.

🏘️
Property Law

Title disputes, possession matters, tenant-landlord issues, property documentation, and real estate legal advisory.

📋
Consumer & Service

Consumer forum complaints, service deficiency matters, insurance disputes, and grievance redressal guidance.

📜
Documentation

Legal notice drafting, affidavits, agreements, MoUs, wills, and notarial services for individuals and businesses.

How It Works

Simple Steps to Get Help

01
Get in Touch

Fill the contact form or WhatsApp with a brief description of your matter.

02
Initial Discussion

A short call or in-person meeting to understand the facts and assess the situation.

03
Legal Opinion

Receive clear, honest advice on your rights, options, and the best path forward.

04
Proceed

If you wish to proceed, the matter is taken up with full dedication and regular updates.

Know Your Rights

Legal Tips & Guidance

General information to help you understand common legal situations. For advice specific to your case, please get in touch directly.

Criminal Law
What to Do After an FIR Is Filed Against You

Stay calm and consult a lawyer immediately. You have the right to a copy of the FIR. Anticipatory bail may be applied for before arrest. Do not make any statement without legal counsel present.

Property
How to Verify Property Title Before Buying

Always check the original chain of title documents for at least 30 years. Verify encumbrance certificate, mutation records, and ensure no pending litigation exists on the property.

Family Law
Rights of Women in Matrimonial Disputes

Women have the right to seek maintenance under Section 125 CrPC, protection under the Domestic Violence Act, and claim matrimonial home rights regardless of whose name the property is in.

Consumer
How to File a Consumer Complaint

Complaints can be filed before District, State, or National Consumer Forums depending on the value involved. Keep all receipts, bills, and communication records. Time limit is two years from the cause of action.

Civil
What Happens When You Receive a Legal Notice

Do not ignore a legal notice. You have 30 days to respond in most cases. Consult an advocate to draft a proper reply. An unanswered notice can be used against you in court as evidence.

General
Your Right to Legal Aid in India

Under Article 39-A of the Constitution and the Legal Services Authorities Act, every person who cannot afford a lawyer has the right to free legal aid. Contact the District Legal Services Authority (DLSA) in your area.
